entry.title: I'm getting the error as "PAN not available" while performing invoice for an indian company code.
entry.content: Please help in resolving this issue.

entry.title: ETag handling in UI5
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I am working with an SAP OData V4 API that uses ETags as an optimistic locking approach. 

I have bound the data to a table but when I change the data and want to update it through the API, no ETag is set in the HTTP request resulting in a 412 error and failing requests. 

I have not found any setting options for this on the model and also no way to set the ETag manually. The only option I have found so far is to completely ignore the ETag (setting a fixed If-Match: * header) and therefore undermining the locking approach.

I can't imagine that ETag handling isn't possible here. Has anyone had any experience with this and can explain to me what needs to be done?

entry.title: Re: A few updates from SAP Business One Support
entry.content:
Hi @JesperB1 

the link doesnt work regardless of sign in with SAP UID or double click.

We simply get the learning framer with message inside: You have not completed all the required content for this course. You must complete the required content in order to confirm completion.

Best regards

Karol

entry.title: Re: Tcodes for below topics
entry.content:
Hi 

Please try to check this

1. Define Employee Group:
Tcode: OOBD

2. Define Employee Subgroup:
Tcode: OOSB

3. Assignment of Personnel Area to Company Code:
Tcode: OBY6

4. Assign Employee Subgroup to Employee Group:
Tcode: OUBD

5. Assign Company Code to Fiscal Year Variant:
Tcode: OB29

6. Assign Company Code to Chart of Accounts:
Tcode: OBD4

7. Define EE Subgroup Grouping for PCR & CAP:
Tcode: OUBD (use this for subgroup grouping)

If you are seeing different screens, it might be due to system authorizations or specific configurations in your SAP instance.Make sure you have the necessary permissions.

entry.title: Re: How to exclude 1 storage location during replenishment run in S//4 Retail
entry.content: Maybe note 2268045 helpshttps://me.sap.com/notes/2268045/E

entry.title: 405 Method Not Allowed when trying HTTP method POST in SAP Gateway Client
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to make a sample of POST data in SEGW that call a BAPI. I follow this tutorial step by step:

Step-by-step Creation of sales order using BAPI in... - SAP Community

However, when I try to POST through SAP Gateway Client, there is error 405 Method Not Allowed. I already tried some solutions including tick Creatable, Updatable in Entity Properties but the error 405 persists.
I also try to create another SEGW project that call a simple BAPI to insert data to my ZTable, but
The similar error 405 also happens

HTTP Status Code : 405
Error Text : The specified HTTP method is not allowed for the resource identified by the Data Service Request URI
T100 Error ID : /IWFND/CM_CONSUMER122

I also make other SEGW sample project to GET data from a BAPI as well, it works well and everything is okay. The problem only happens when I POST data to a BAPI create.
Is there any solution for this error? Is is related to networking setting/configuration?

entry.title: Re: Digital Manufacturing - EWM Integration settings
entry.content:
Hello,

In the customized table for defining the synchronization of stock data with MES, you need to provide the entries based on the configurations in your system, including:

• PSA in ERP

• ERP system

• Plant

• Warehouse number

• Supply area

• Synchronize stock data : Ensure is synchronized correctly. The entry should be labeled as "Synchronized."

Best regard

Thanks for the URL. I checked it but it's not working. Is there any other way to download?
https://solace.com/integration-hub/sap-integration-suite/ 

Thanks,
Sakshi

entry.title: SAP PaPM On-Premise: Exporting Environments
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

With the emergence of SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud sometime 3 years ago against the backdrop of a constantly evolving On-Premise solution, those who have already established a great deal of their calculations in On-Premise intended for PoC’s, demos or for any other valuable purpose might probably have the intention of preserving these important environments and reuse them at some point in Cloud, perhaps to consider switching over or for starters, just to explore the intricacies in Cloud.

Having the concept of an unnecessary reconfiguration of the entire model from ground up but at the same time guiding users when it comes to required changes in modeling along with other considerations, SAP Profitability and Performance Management has designed exactly the right applications enabling the convenient and efficient export and import of important environments from On-Premise to Cloud by way of the Export Environment (On-Premise) and Content Management (Cloud) applications, hence, making these important environments reusable from one platform to another.

This blogpost intends to present from an On-Premise standpoint, the export side of the export-import concept which will be made possible through the Export Environment application. So, if the application is still quite new to you or you belong to those who have already tried it but there are still some areas which you are not totally familiar with – I’ve got you covered.

Meanwhile, the Content Management application is a totally different topic of which relevant information is provided separately in another blogpost (see SAP PaPM Cloud: Introducing the New Content Management Screen).

Export Environment Application

Introduced as one of the new enhancements via On-Premise’s SP17 release, the application is found and can be launched via SAP Menu à Profitability and Performance Management à Administration à Export Environment or via transaction code /NXI/P1_FW_EXP_ENV.

 On the Export Environment screen, the only required information are the following:

  • Env.+Version (EEEVVVV) which stands for the environment and version
  • Directory for Download which can be any local folder where the compressed .json files will be placed

The application is capable of "exporting" or the compression of a chosen environment into a collection of .json files enclosed in a file Folder, saved in a preferred local directory.

 

 

 

 

 

Note: This file will then be used by the Content Management application to extract the environment during its import procedure and make it available in Cloud.

More than the application’s export functionality, it further comes with built-in consistency check which essentially acts like a “triage” – further driving the main objective of only exporting consistent environments down the line which in principle, is meeting the following requirements:

  • All functions are active.
  • Configurations and settings are supported in Cloud.

Consistency checks ensure that all functions are active. Hence, corrections in environment setup, settings and configurations are ensured first by the user to guarantee that each environment for export is properly configured and consistent.

Consistency Checks

A chosen environment to be exported is subjected to the following checks, ensuring the said environment’s reusability in PaPM Cloud. Only when an environment has undergone and satisfied these consistency checks shall it be successfully compressed to a zip file and for that matter, considered to be successfully exported.

Otherwise, the Export Environment application displays message logs:

  • A Warning in case the system detects indicators of a badly designed environment which would have an impact in terms of performance.
  • An Error pointing out inconsistencies, guiding the user further when it comes to the required modifications before the environment can be re-exported.
Warning messages

In PaPM modeling, there are generally quite a number of considerations to have a so-called optimized calculation model/modeling environment, including HANA DB parameters, correct sizing, volume of data involved in certain calculations, materialization of data involving just the right number of functions, and so on, to name a few.

Within the Export Environment application, the runtime performance may be impacted by sub-optimal modeling, and so the system gives information to the user that essentially implies for possible improvements in re-designing the model:

  • An increasing number of functions > (greater than) 200
  • Fields > (greater than) 1,000
  • A Join function containing > (greater than) 15 rules 

Error Messages

The consistency check as it was stated helps ensure that all functions are activated for a specific environment being considered for export. Since the concept of export from PaPM On-Premise would imply the environment being imported to PaPM Cloud at a later time, the consistency checks also block further unsupported data sources, fields, connections, and functions.

  • Inactive functions

  • Function type set to ‘Default’ is not permitted, and is blocked by the application.

Note: This is applicable to all functions (Allocation, Calculation, Transfer Structure, Join, View and Writer) with a corresponding function type setting in their Header section

For example, Allocation is the prescribed setting instead of the Default (Allocation).

Otherwise, the corresponding error log is displayed:

  • External data sources except for Model Table HANA is supported, but should be used with Connection Type “Logical”

  • Only environment fields and HANA fields are supported; hence, the application displays the corresponding error logs for other fields:

Sample error for BW fields

Sample error for BW InfoObjects

Sample error for DDIC fields

Sample error for Environment InfoObject fields

  • Unsupported functions

These are just some of the functions which are generally not supported in PaPM Cloud, and thus blocked by the application.

Sample error for Unsupported Functions

In a nutshell, the Export Environment application carries out the following consistency checks:

 

Prerequisites

Authorization

The necessary authorization for the Export Environment application is assigned to role /NXI/P1_ADMIN_USER_ALL.

SAP Notes

A list of notes are to be considered to take advantage of the application’s functionality along with its enhancements not available during the time of its actual release.

  • For SP16 and below, the following notes should be implemented along with its manual post implementation steps, if provided in the Manual Activities section of the note. 
    • SAP Note 3138297 - FS-PER Rel. 3.0 SP17: Export Environment
    • SAP Note 3209329 - FS-PER Rel. 3.0 SP18: Minor Improvements for Export Environment
    • SAP Note 3322703 - LOD-ANA-PER-OP 3.0 SP22: Export Environment Enhancements
    • SAP Note 3369372 - LOD-PER-OP 3.0 SP23: Export Environment, adjust Checks
  • For SP17 onwards, implement the notes as they are applicable.

There you have it. Feel free to share this blogpost with other colleagues in case you find it helpful. Should you have questions, feel free to raise them through https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html using the primary tag “SAP Profitability and Performance Management”.

You may also check out other interesting blogposts and other relevant information about SAP Profitability and Performance Management in the SAP Community page: https://community.sap.com/topics/profitability-and-performance-management 

Thanks a lot for your time and attention. Until next time, everyone!

Khan

entry.title: Export to spreadsheet to be enabled for ALV Tree
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I made an ALV Tree and want to enable the export to spreadsheet option. For ALV Grid display the option is enabled but it's not the case for ALV Trees. 

Screenshot 1: Export option available for Grid Display

Screenshot 2: Export option not available. All other print options are also disabled.

SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP for SAP Business Suite

entry.title: BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE is working in SE37 but is only partially working through program
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

This is my first post so I might not come across very clearly. Please bear with me.

I am using 'BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE' to create a PR in EBAN table, which also contains ADRNR/ADRN2 fields.

My requirement states that the ADRNR/ADRN2 field should get auto-generated in the record once the BAPI is executed, and I will then update the same ADRNR into ADRC table manually.

I read that the prerequisite for this requirement is that the REQUISITION_ADDRDELIVERY structure should be populated, and I am doing the same.

However, once the BAPI is executed through my program, the PR is generated with empty ADRNR/ADRN2 fields. If I execute the same BAPI through SE37 using the same inputs, the ADRNR field is being populated.

Any ideas on where I might be going wrong?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: User Set Status Change CJ20N or CJ02
entry.content: Thank you for the advice, this issue is solved.😊

entry.title: Re: User Set Status Change CJ20N or CJ02
entry.content: Thank you for your reply, I used the BADI WORKBREAKDOWN_UPDATE.

entry.title: SAP Dispute Management - Create a new dispute case
entry.content:
Hello together,

we have following problem, what is curios:

When we create a new dispute case above the transaction code FBL5N, then we can't see in the transaction code UDM_DISPUTE the right employees and the external reference number. 

BUT, when we go to the transaction code UDM_DISPUTE and open the dispute case and click on the button EDIT and SAVE, then the sap system shows automatically the right employees and the external reference number. The SAP System pulls the data, that is curios.

My understanding is, when we created the new dispute case from the FBL5N, then the sap system automatically must show the right employees and external reference number in the transaction code UDM_DISPUTE.    

The data would have to be drawn automatically.

Can someone support us?

Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards,

L. E.

entry.title: special Keyfigure calculation in BW query designer
entry.content:
When calculating a key figure (Net value YTM), the sales of all months up to the month that is currently displayed in the outline should be summed up.

How would such a key figure calculation have to be defined in the query designer ?

see attachment

thx, br oliver

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite Application Not Opening in Trial "Unsubscription Failed" Error 5

entry.title: Negative sign in front for Numeric Data Type
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

I have an ALV Report that needs to be downloaded. Since the export option is disabled, I am exporting the internal table instead and used the GUI_DOWNLOAD function module. 

When downloaded the negative signs are coming at the end of the numbers. I used the CLOI_PUT_SIGN_IN_FRONT but since I used Type P decimals 0, the types are not compatible. Is there any other way to specify the placement of the negative sign?

Any suggestions/solutions will be really appreciated.

When downloaded:

SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP

entry.title: Get Cycle Time between two events in Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I would like to get the cycle time between two events in Process Intelligence. I think I can do this by putting in two filters on CT widget. 

  1. Put an activity event type filter and select those events 
  2. Put a behaviour filter with A~>B 

This ensures that both the events are present in the filtered cases and only those two events are present in those case. 

Is there a more efficient way of doing this ? 
In Celonis, we just have to select 2 events in a single filter and it works.

entry.title: Re: A Beginner's demo for pushing data from SAP CPI to SOLACE
entry.content:
@Sakshi_T - I have just used the same URL to download. Seems like its down now.

Meanwhile, you can work with Advanced event mesh adapter on trial system. 
It has exactly same options to be configured as that of SOLACE adapter.

AEM adapter could also be used to connect with SOLACE, but its is not the best approach. Since its just hands-on check, you could give it a try.

Thanks,

Rohini Potham

entry.title: The transaction code UDM_AUTOWRITEOFF don't close the dispute case
entry.content:
Hello together,

can you tell us, why the program don't close the dispute case?

We define the right reason code and escalation reason and save the transaction:

After the transaction, we go to the transaction code UDM_AUTOWRITEOFF and select the right data. The programm run and show this answer:

BUT, the dispute case must be close, that means, that the amount must be 0,00 EUR and the status must be "CLOSE". But the sap system don't close the dispute case and i can see the open amount and the old status.

What is the problem? I've checked also the customizing, all is fine. The G/L account and cost center is available. 

Can someone help us? Thank you so muss for the support.

Best regards,

L. E.

hello,

did you solve this issue? could you please provide me the solution you got 

thanks

entry.title: Re: Export to spreadsheet to be enabled for ALV Tree
entry.content:
Didn't you already find blogs such as SALV Tree to Excel (xlsx) 

  • Basically, convert the tree displayed data to an internal table and use grid alv export tools
  • You could also request this feature from SAP via Customer Influence (You're not in a hurry, are you?)

entry.title: Re: RAP: Creating a child entity with 'create by associat...
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

wow, thanks for the super fast response and for the provided snippets!!

It works like a charm. Those were the issues why it didn't work for me:

  • The EML create statement was missing the "auto fill cid" part
  • I had [1] instead of [1..*] in the action definition
    • When creating an associated entity the cardinality of [1] does work (the entity gets created), but after creation the generated app tries to navigate to the newly created root instance (which does not exist, since we did not create any)
    • Here a screenshot of the ABAP doc, maybe the part with "factory action always need to have a cardinality of [1]" could get adjusted

 

Thanks again for your awesome support and have a nice day!
Daniel

entry.title: Email to be sent to email ID if document is released from Legal control block
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Please let us know if there is any way we can send an email if document is released from Legal control block similar to the functionality where email is send when document is blocked for Legal control check.

Suggest any FM or BADI which can be used to achieve the same.

entry.title: Re: liberation strategy connection
entry.content: Please use standard flex workflow scenarios in S4 if they exists. Please see last picture Migration Options - when to do what: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/which-workflow-engine-s-for-which-workflow-solution/ba-p/13507469

entry.title: Re: Plant Maintenance module without HR(SAP HCM)
entry.content:
Hi!

In short: yes, there are ways to do that.

In a more detailed fashion: as always, it depends on what you're after 🙂

First of all, let me point out different levels regarding the assignment of work.

  1. on work center level, only considering general availability/capacity provided by your workforce. Like this, you don't even have to consider the individual persons and just make sure that you have enough capacity available for your work.
  2. via "person responsible": on operation level you can make a 1:1 one connection between a person and an operation, e.g. in Tx IW32 (or the respective Fiori app) or in our Asset Management for resource scheduling solution (RSH)
  3. and finally creating 1:n assignments between an operation and multiple executing technicians, for which there are also mutliple ways
    1. creating "capacity splits" with RSH
    2. assigning technicians via SAP Field Service Management (FSM)
    3. manually creating capacity splits within an operation via Iw32, see details in the respective SAP Help for onPremise/Private Cloud Edition here.

For options 2 and 3, there is a documentation regarding assigning individuals to operations via RSH, which can be found here:
S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/bd80e60bd9d2448ba31ad0d888d8e264.html?locale=en-US

S/4HANA onPrem/Private Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/bd80e60bd9d2448ba31ad0d888d8e264.html?locale=en-US

In FSM, there are multiple concepts, you might want to explore:

  • Dispatching to a technician via the Dispatching Board as well as automatic schedule filling for a technician 
  • Supporting Technician
  • Dispatching to Crews and Teams

Hope this helps!

Regards,

Markus

entry.title: ABAP RAP: How to call the default function with multiple instance
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need to add some specify logic to determine default value with multiple instance, only if all selected instances have the same status, then the default value can be determined, otherwise, the value should be empty.
I have added annotation @ui.lineItem: [{ type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'ChangeStatus', invocationGrouping: #CHANGE_SET }] in metadata extensions, I found that all methods are called as a group except the default function.

How could I implement this requirement?

Best Regards,
Wei

entry.title: Adding fields in standard report in public cloud (MB52)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

we have one requirement in our current project to add few fields in a report. 

Fiori app : MB52/Display warehouse stock

There are few fields like manufacture date, vendor batch number, expiry bond date (that is available in classification tab in batch information cockpit)

Any idea how we can add these fields in the standard report.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Any input on this is highly appreciated.

Regards,

Jagadhatri

entry.title: Re: Exclude Payment Method In Payment Run for Suppliers
entry.content: Hi!

entry.title: Re: Exclude Payment Method In Payment Run for Suppliers
entry.content:
Hi!

This works, but creates an issue when processing the bank statement.

The debit line from the supplier direct debit can be matched to an open supplier invoice in AP, but since there is a warning due to the Payment Block, ByD will not automatically allocate the item and this needs to be confirmed manually by the user.

Any other ideas?

Thanks,
Eelco

entry.title: Re: Certificate Expire Notifications Alert in SAP Cloud Integration - iFlow
entry.content: @Sathish_Kumar_BP  I think it is a useful iFlow, and you deserve praise for sharing it, but I'm afraid you could have saved yourself some work by using the iFlow for the same task that we can find in the community section of the Business Accelerator Hub (also known as the API Hub). See it in SAP Cloud Integration Utilities package

entry.title: Re: Automation with the btp and cf command-line interfaces: logging in with passcodes
entry.content:
Dear @tom_slee , 

we use this approach in shell scripts to authenticate against CF orgs and BTP subaccounts using a technical user. I think this is a big step forward in terms of security as this approach avoids using a shared secret (the password) in combination with technical users. 
Do you know whether this approach may work also in other scenarios where technical users are widely used?
For instance, Automation Pilot use cases like starting/stopping/patching HANA Cloud instance or in the SAP Cloud Transport Management (to transport MTAs in CF landscapes)?
In all these use cases we need to provide a technical user with password and the technical user has to be a Space Developer in the CF space. Actually, we would like to replace this authentication via user/password by authentication via X.509 certificates. 
 
Thanks and regards, 
  Alexander

entry.title: B2B Integration Factory – B2B related Integration Packages with your Custom Extensions
entry.content:
Introduction

The article B2B Integration Factory services to unlock the power of SAP Integration Suite's B2B capabilities explains that the B2B Integration Factory publishes integration packages with extensions especially for the Trading Partner Management capability within SAP Integration Suite. These include customer-requested enhancements that are not yet available in the product standard in the short to medium term. We're pleased to announce that you’ll find a range of B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages with your extensions developed by the B2B Integration Factory in the SAP Accelerator Hub’s Community section. But it’s not just about getting the requested B2B integration features sooner. There are further advantages for you in comparison to your own made customizations:

  1. Consistent and sustainable implementation with a clear path to standard once available (Clean core).
  2. The extensions are generic and reusable as possible. For e.g. an extension for enriching functional acknowledgements will work for all kind functional acknowledgements such as UN/EDIFACT and ASC X12.
  3. The B2B Integration Factory gives you support via the GitHub

 

The aim of B2B Integration Factory - Integration Packages with Custom Extensions

The product development team continuously enhances the B2B capabilities within SAP Integration Suite in line with the published SAP Product Roadmap, but these may not be available at the time the customer requires this additional feature. The Trading Partner Management system itself is based on an integration package called Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management V2 with a set of generic integration flows for processing and exchanging the B2B data accordingly. This integration package is centrally available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Typically, many of the such as additional enriching of functional acknowledgements can be met in the short term by extending these extension integration flows. These are typical extensions ,in where the standard flows don't provide such extension points for compliance, consistency and support reasons.

Now, developing proprietary extensions on the customer side is a burden in terms of ownership, maintenance, and the lack of support or updates. That's why we, the B2B Integration Factory, in close collaboration with product development, have decided to provide you with our own central set of B2B integration packages with the required extensions. Starting from today, we’re going to publish every month a new release of these B2B integration packages with additional extensions at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub’s Community section. This has the following advantages for you as user of the B2B capabilities within the SAP Integration Suite:

  1. No rank growth of different locally developed integration flows for the same feature extensions
  2. Support by the B2B Integration Factory via the underlying GitHub
  3. Provision of updates with further extensions quickly and effectively every month
  4. Close alignment of these extensions with product development for consistency and compliance purposes

In detail, this means for you:

 

1. No Rank Growth

As a B2B Integration Factory, we collect, consolidate, and prioritize the extension requirements from our customer projects. We analyze whether this requirement can be implemented into our set of integration flows on short notice using the methods we can use, such as enhancements to the integration flows, or via additional Groovy script, JavaScript, XSLT, and so on. If this is the case, we implement the additional extension in accordance with our conventions and guidelines. We use our own regression tests framework to check whether this extension works properly with the existing standard features or the previously implemented enhancements and does not have any negative effects or sidekicks. If this is the case, we will then make the enhancements available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub together with HTTP-based test cases and implementation documentation in one of the next releases. This central approach enables us to offer you a consistent and tested solution of enhancements that do not have any limitations or issues during productive operation.

 

2. Limited and GitHub based Support

However, if a bug or issue occurs, we offer limited support via the associated GitHub project. You can raise an issue using the "Issue" button in the respective GitHub entry of the related integration package. Restricted support means:

  1. No SAP official support will be triggered, and the corresponding SLA does not apply for this either.
  2. Only the B2B Integration Factory team provides support during their working hours. This may mean that the reaction time will take a few hours.
  3. Troubleshooting refers only to the extended integration flows and scripts provided by the B2B Integration Factory.
    1. A direct support cannot be provided, if an integration flow has been further adjusted by the user.
    2. If the root cause analysis determines that this is a bug in one of the standard features, the customer will be asked to create an official SAP support ticket.
  4. All error messages are listed centrally in the GitHub and can be seen by the other users.

All the patches with fixed errors will be made available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub’s Community section in a timely manner. The possible delivery date will be reported accordingly.

 

3. Monthly Provisioning of Extensions

As already communicated, the B2B Integration Factory keeps a list of requested features. It consolidates and prioritizes them every month. Usually, these requirements come from customer projects. However, you also have the option of communicating new requirements using the “Pull Request” button of the respective entry of the integration package in GitHub.

All these submitted requests are checked for feasibility and considered in the monthly prioritization. The more users vote for an entry, the higher this entry is prioritized for the next release.

 The B2B Integration Factory also ensures that these requested extensions are also forwarded to standard product. This also applies to requested extensions that cannot be implemented by the B2B Integration Factory directly. To expedite the implementation process, the B2B Integration Factory will ask the customer to enter or vote for the feature enhancement on the SAP Continuous Influence page of SAP Integration Suite, if required. The B2B Integration Factory then agrees with product development as to when this feature request will be included in the standard product. The customer is then informed accordingly or will be informed about the updated SAP Product Roadmap.

 

4. Close Alignment with Product Development

One of the enormous advantages of the B2B Integration Factory is the close collaboration with product development. We not only have a direct influence on prioritization, but we are also very much involved in the product development process. We ensure that feature requirements are implemented in the standard product according to our implementation in the corresponding B2B Integration Factory - Integration Packages.  As we try to make the standard implementation as compatible as possible with the B2B Integration Factory made extension, we guarantee that the resulting migration path does not entail any significant effort.

Once a corresponding feature is covered by the standard product, we remove this feature from the integration flows in our B2B Integration Factory - Integration Packages and develop a final migration path. The steps required for this migration are described in a specific how-to guide.

 

Conclusion

Ultimately, this approach ensures that you can implement your additional B2B integration requirements sooner rather than later. In addition, close collaboration with product development ensures that these requested features are implemented in the standard product in a way that meets your end requirements and minimizes the effort required to migrate from the extended integration flows to the standard product.

The next blog will show you which enhancements will be included in the first release of the B2B Integration Factory - Integration Packages.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Active/Inactive Versions
entry.content: Thanks Jef! What I have noticed is that these inactive versions can't be managed via the versions panel. If the user adds the model to a table and checks the versions, they don't display as private versions. I have submitted an incident on the SAP Support Portal as well to get some more clarity. If anything useful comes out of that, I will share it here as well.

entry.title: Data migration from SAP B1 to S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are currently planning on the Data Migration project to migrate data from SAP Business One(B1) to S4HANA public cloud.

In the below link Migration options between SAP B1 and S4HANA Public Cloud 

@Yong_Yu  mentioned "you can use S/4 Migration Cockpit to migrate legacy data from B1 HANA table to S/4 directly". I have the following questions regarding this statement.

  1. Does the above statement mean to use the Migration cockpit(Fiori App ID: F3473) to download the corresponding master/transaction data Templates, fill the templates and then upload these templates to the cockpit, to complete the migration process ? or any other alternative suggested here ?
  2. If using data templates, I would like to know, whether there are any alternatives available to automate the data transfer from SAP B1 to these templates or are these templates supposed to be filled done manually ?

Thank you!

Regards,
Karthik

I would suggest to try the end to end scenario in your Quality system. During BoM explosion in Sales Order. may be the Plant for Components is copied from Header and user will have to change Plant for respective component manually. 

However in your position I would test the overall process including Financial postings. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Is there a way I can incorporate the results on BO Query Builder into a webi report?

Please help on this. 

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP C4C User ID - Indexed and Searchable
entry.content:
Within SAP C4C, our USER ID  has been have mapped to our inbound CDSID (Corporate Directory System ID) which is our Login username i.e. JSMITH21.  Mapped between SuccessFactors and using CPI.

When we come to Employee records we cannot search for a Employees CDSID (User ID).

Is there a way to expose User ID i.e. make it an indexed field that is searchable?

Thanks,
Andrew

the my global account is an "Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP" and I have activated Joule in SAP Build Code.

 

Thanks,

Andrea

entry.title: Re: BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE is working in SE37 but is onl...
entry.content:
Usually, if it works with SE37 in test mode and not in a program, it's often due to a data format error.

  • SE37 uses conversion exits attached to domains, to convert a readable/external format into an internal format
  • The program passes the data as received
  • Check your data format, and use FMs such as CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT to correct wrong values.
  • Call BAPI_REQUISITION_GETDETAIL to get correct formats from an existing data

entry.title: Re: How do we customize the header name on Learning ILX n...
entry.content:
Hi @VaibhavModi , 

the text is stored as a value for a specific label. Make sure to have user frontend and administration set to the same language. Then, in learning administration, go to References -> Manage Labels -> Labels and select 
'Label Value' as search filter. Then, simply enter the text you wana change and click on search. The more of the text you enter, the best result you get. Click on the label and switch to 'locales' tab and make your changes. Do not forget to save and then you are good to go 🙂

We notices, that labels for the mobile app are not stored as label values in learning administration, but as label in plattform where you need to setup custom language value per language. 

Cheers, 
Marc

entry.title: How can I add more Collaboration Links in DMC and Collaboration Directives in DMC?
entry.content:
We have connected our S/4 Hana Cloud system to our DMC. 

We used communication arrangements for information from S/4 to DMC.

But to bring information from DMC to S/4 the documentation says we need to map Collaboration Links to Directives. But the Collaboration Links the documentation is referring to, aren't in the DMC system. 

Also the Directives the documentation is referring to aren't in the system. I can also no create new Links or Directives. I do have the correct Role Collection in the BTP (manufacturing_admin).

Can someone help?

entry.title: Exciting Updates in Our Latest Newsletter - Check Out Bulletin for Oil, Gas, and Energy!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the release of the SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter - September Edition!
Packed with valuable insights, this edition features inspiring stories from industry leaders like Petrobras and Equinor, highlighting their innovative strides in technology and sustainability. Discover how Oil India Limited is enhancing its operations with RISE with SAP and learn about Shin Pyeongtaek Power Co.’s groundbreaking cloud-based ERP solutions.

 

Don’t miss news about our events, including the SLB Digital Forum in Monaco, ASUG Best Practices in Houston, and ADIPEC in Abu Dhabi—your chance to engage with experts and explore the latest industry innovations. Dive into our NEW in-depth articles and catch up on the recent SAP for Energy & Utilities Espresso Sessions for actionable insights.

Stay informed and connected—check out the full newsletter here!
Subscribe now to receive the next newsletter directly in your e-mail box.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano

I'm a SAP ABAP Developer from India. I've been working in SAP ABAP development on various modules like PP PM QM HR SD MM for 10 years now. Currently I am on a career break due to maternity leave and want to upgrade myself with the new SAP BTP certification and join back the Industry. I identified as a person for this certification because I am a women, first generation college graduate in my family, People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts, Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location.

I really loved working with SAP because it helped businesses improve their processes. It's amazing to see how technology can make such a big difference. I want to keep learning and growing in this field to have a great career.

I'm eager to take a free certification attempt because I believe it will help me learn more and become even better at what I do. I want to stay up-to-date with the latest advancements.I am grateful for this opportunity and excited about the possibilities ahead. Thank you for considering my application for the free certification attempt.

Thanks & Regards,

Pearl (Pallavi Nanoskar)

entry.title: ABAP RAP: Is there a way to filter with additional binding fields with empty value in Value Help
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to implement an 'Old Status' dropdown list and a 'New Status' dropdown list.
The 'New Status' is dependent on 'Old Status'.
If the 'Old Status' is empty, the 'New Status' should be empty.
I have added the following annotations, it works for the 'Old Status' with a non-empty value, but doesn't work with 'Old Status' with an empty value.

I have tried with value help with parameters, but it doesn't work, the parameters can not be bounded in Fiori elements by rap CDS.

Is there a way to filter the empty value?

entry.title: Sybase IQ 16.1 OLEDB driver problem with SQL Server Integration Services / Visual Studio
entry.content:
Hello,

I have identified a critical issue with the latest OLEDB Sybase IQ driver. The issue occurs intermittently and does not follow a predictable pattern.

I am using OLEDB driver in our Visual Studio Data projects (SQL Server Integration Services - SSIS) to execute Sybase IQ stored procedures or transfer data from Sybase IQ as the source to destination tables (SQL Server).

The issue occurs during debugging of the VS project or when running via SQL Server Agent (job):

  • Sometimes, the task using Sybase IQ connection finishes successfully, but no data is processed or the procedure is not executed
  • One part of SSIS workflow is processed, but the subsequent steps are skipped, and the workflow pretends everything is done
  • Running jobs fail due to a problem with Sybase IQ driver

Can anyone to help to solve this issue. Thanks.

System information:

  • Sybase IQ Database Version: SAP IQ/16.1.040.2016/17091/P/SP04.14/MS/Windows 2012 R2/64bit
  • Sybase IQ Driver: dboledb17.dll (ver. 17.0.11.2690)
  • Operating System: Windows Server 2022 Standard ver. 21H2 build 20348.2655 (where SQL Server and Visual Studio run)
  • Microsoft Visual Studio: Professional 2022 64bit ver. 17.10.4 (.NET Framework 4.8.09037)
  • Microsoft SQL Server: Developer 64bit (ver. 16.0.1125.1)

entry.title: Re: fiori elements : List Report aggregate function
entry.content:
@inesmartins 

For the "feature is not supported error", please add the following configuration to package.json.

  "cds": {
    "features": {
      "odata_new_parser": true
    }
  }

I have written the following blog post on this topic.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/fiori-elements-analytical-table-with-cap-does-it-work-with-v2-and-v4/ba-p/13565008

entry.title: Substance Volume Tracking Block Purchase Orders S/HANA Cloud Public Edition - Product Compliance
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently working with a customer implementing the substance volumen tracking 4OL best practice. Now the question occured if there is any standard possibility to block a purchase order when reaching the managed limits of substances. 

As far as I know from the SAP help plattform the management of the limits is only via Monitor Substance Volumes. Is there any feature without development (planned?) to block postings when limits are exceeded?

Maybe you can help @Michael_Schalk?

Kind regards

Michael

entry.title: How to find/analyze materials that have formulas applied on their BOMs?

We are using formulas on BOMs (applied through the recipe) to calculate BOM-quantities.  We want to know which items do have formulas applied, but as these are not stored in a table, it is difficult to analyze.

Any idea how they can be found easily?

Thx,

Fabian

entry.title: hello
Re: Add custom field to F0718 - Post General Journal Entr...

Hello. We currently have also a requirement to add a custom field in the app F0718.

Our configuration is a On Premise S/4 Hana 2023 FPS1.

We did exactly the same as @saurabh__khare. We are able to see the custom field added in the table BSEG but when trying to use the Adapt UI, we are not allowed to add anything on the "item details block". 

@saurabh__khare did you solved the issue on your side ?

@AndreasMuno, unfortunately, the documentation you mentioned does not help to solve the issue at all. It should work but in the case of this app (F0718), we are stuck. 

On our side, we are about to check how to add our custom field in the app F0718 with the use of VS Code. If you have any advice, I'd love to hear from you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Readiness Check 2.0 for SAP S/4HANA - FAQ & What's New in Releases
entry.content: Hi, when uploading data to the SAP readiness check cloud application, is the data encrypted thru TLS? Any documentation on this? Thanks!

entry.title: TextNow Number Lookup by iStaunch – Track TextNow Number for Free
entry.content: To track who owns a TextNow number, open go to iStaunch.com and TextNow Number Lookup by iStaunch. Enter the number and tap on the track button. That’s it, you see the real identity of the TextNow number holder including details such as name, phone number, email id, location, IP address, and much more.

entry.title: Re: SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors Solutions – Now in Pilot Phase (until GA 9/2022)
entry.content: Hi @astridtschense when uploading data to the SAP readiness check cloud application, is the data encrypted thru TLS? Any documentation on this? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Foreign Currency valuation
entry.content:
Hi 

Please check the below Advance FX valuation in S4 HANA versus Classic Revaluation:

1. Valuation Rules for GL Groups: In S4 HANA, you can use semantic tags in Financial Statement Versions (FSVs) to create flexible reporting. However, setting up different valuation rules for GL groups may require additional configuration compared to the straightforward valuation areas in Classic FX.

2. Custom Field Assignments: Activating custom fields for FX valuation can be complex. Unlike Classic valuation, where specific options are more straightforward, S4 HANA may involve more development work to meet unique business requirements.

3. Inheritance of Profit Centers: Inheritance of profit centers for FX gain/loss accounts generally works similarly in S4 HANA, but ensure proper account determination settings are in place to maintain accuracy.

4. Document Types for Valuation Postings: Segregating document types for valuation postings is limited in S4 HANA. You may need to implement alternative strategies to differentiate postings.

5. Additional Challenges: Common challenges include adapting to new configurations, ensuring proper integration with other modules, and understanding the impact of new features on existing processes.

entry.title: Re: Time out error with Build process automation while running GUI screen recording
entry.content: Hello Pawan, Thank you for reaching out to us. Based on the information provided, it seems like server-side scripting may not have been enabled. Could you please double-check this ( https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/enabling-scripting-on-client-side?locale=en-US&q=ecc+scripting) ( https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/enabling-scripting-on-server-side?locale=en-US&q=ecc+scripting ) and let us know if you're still facing the issue?

entry.title: Re: BADI ManageEmailSubjectPrefix implementation

Thanks for the explanation. 

I have a requirement to create a new ticket when existing ticket is closed and I tried adding a dummy value in Ticket Number and Offset as "1" from the badi but it still falls in unassociated mails. Is there any other thing which I'm missing here? Could you please help me with this.

 

Regards,

AT

entry.title: Re: Hi i want to download the Recent PCE Rule set of S4/H...
entry.content:
Hi

You can find the latest PCE ruleset for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition in the SAP Help Portal. Here's the link to get you started:

[SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition Documentation](https://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_S4HANA_PRIVATE_CLOUD_EDITION/)

Navigate to the relevant section for PCE guidelines or rulesets

entry.title: How to enable Country Specific Pricing Procedure in Service Contracts
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

Can you please guide us as to how to enable Country Specific Pricing Procedures regarding Service Contracts?

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to enable Country Specific Pricing Procedure in S...
entry.content:
The Pricing Procedure is determined from Sales-Org based on the customizing and the tax can be controlled by the Ship-to-Party determined Tax Destination Country or manually changed Tax Destination Country. The Tax Destination Country is located in the billing assignment block (Billing tab).

For the Pricing Procedure determination, please kindly check SSCUI 101118 (Set Pricing Procedure Determination) and check the Pricing Procedure maintained for Sales-Org and Document Type S3 for Service Contract.

entry.title: Re: Retrieve remaining quantity of product
entry.content:
Hello @KhaingZinTun 

A material availability according to ATP logic can be retrieved with Product Availability Info API.

Whereas, a material current stock can be read with Material Stock - Read API.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Empowering the Future of Utilities: Highlights from 2024 SAP for Utilities Event
entry.content: Hi Katie - I cannot believe I didn't see you last week.  Thank you for a great recap.  It was a great event.
